frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Girls, this is so exhausting. I told a guy very clearly I wasn’t interested, and he kept pushing till he found out I have a boyfriend — suddenly then it became ‘oh, I respect that.’ Apparently my no alone was not enough, but some other man’s presence made it valid.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it describes a normalized attitude where a woman’s refusal is treated as negotiable until another man’s claim is established. It encodes the surface manifestation of stopping pursuit only after learning she is ‘taken,’ not after her direct rejection.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 29. Single. A woman working in an MNC in Pune. You have been told by your female manager informally that you should make more effort with your appearance because client facing roles require a certain standard of presentation. You have never worn much makeup. You are posting anonymously in a women only WhatsApp group after this conversation sharing what your manager said and how it made you feel and the impossible position women are put in Ñ judged for wearing too much and judged for wearing too little.
